tasks.title: Define backlog schema
acceptance: Backlog item IDs use `BL-001` style identifiers and are unique.
Runtime backlog data remains under `work/backlog/backlogs.json`, not `.harness/`.

checks: architecture and design docs describe `.harness/schemas/backlogs.schema.json`, `.harness/templates/backlogs.template.json`, and `work/backlog/backlogs.json` consistently.
verification evidence is present and relevant
schema, template, and design docs are synchronized
workflow and task state changes used Harness gateways

tasks.title: Implement backlog intake gateway
acceptance: The script creates `work/backlog/backlogs.json` from the template when the store is missing.
The script appends a new item with the next available `BL-NNN` ID.
The script accepts `title`, `summary`, `dispatch`, `sourceRef`, optional `notes`, and optional `createdAt`.
The script validates the full backlog store before atomic write.
The script never writes `workflow-state.json`, `tasks.json`, or active plan files.

checks: repeated intake calls produce sequential IDs and preserve existing items.
verification evidence is present and relevant
intake gateway validates existing and next backlog stores
atomic write is scoped to work/backlog/backlogs.json
workflow-state, tasks, and active plan files are not mutated

tasks.title: Wire backlog intake rules and CLI
acceptance: `session-start.py` includes backlog schema, template, rule, and script in required Harness assets.
`backlog-rules.md` states that `queue` records normal follow-up work and `preempt` requests evaluation, but neither mutates the active workflow.
Documentation identifies backlog intake as intake-side runtime data, separate from active plan execution.

checks: CLI delegates backlog-intake through the unified harness entrypoint
session-start preflight requires backlog intake assets
backlog-rules documents queue/preempt without active workflow mutation
documentation separates intake runtime data from active plan execution
